subject: GRB 160225.81/FERMI Trigg 478121069: MASTER OT J095503.19-355149.4 follow up observations
submitter: Vladimir Lipunov at Moscow State U/Krylov Obs  <lipunov@xray.sai.msu.ru>
body:
D.Buckley, S. Potter, M.Kotze
South African Astronomical Observatory

N.Tyurina, E. Gorbovskoy, V. Lipunov, V.Kornilov, P.Balanutsa, A.Kuznetsov, 
D.Kuvshinov
Lomonosov Moscow State University,
Sternberg Astronomical Institute, Moscow State University

A.Gabovich, V.Yurkov, Yu.Sergienko, D.Varda
Blagoveschensk Educational State University, Blagoveschensk

K.Ivanov, N.M.Budnev, O.Gres, V.A.Poleshchuk
Irkutsk State University

A. Tlatov, V.Senik, A.V. Parhomenko, D. Dormidontov
Kislovodsk Solar Station of the Pulkovo Observatory

R. Rebolo, M. Serra-Ricart, N. Lodieu, G. Israelian
The Instituto de Astrofisica de Canarias


MASTER-SAAO reobserved MASTER OT J095503.19-355149.4. The OT 
becomes brighter on 0.8 during ~1day, m_OT=17.3 on 2016-02-26 18:44:29.023 
(from 18.1 on 2016-02-25 21:43:00.902UT)

So this OT is not connected with FERMI GBM TRIGGER NUM 478121069 (possible 
dwarf nova, amplitude more then 4.7mag)


The message may be cited.
